title: Lastrea athamantica
intro: Lastrea athamantica, also known as the Athamantus or Athamantus fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain, improve wound healing, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Lastrea athamantica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other polyphenolic compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, skin disorders, and digestive issues.
uses: Lastrea athamantica is a species of fern, but it does not appear to have well-documented medicinal uses. benefits: Lastrea athamantica, also known as the Athamanthus fern, is a plant species that is traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties.
